title: Soulangia thymifolia
intro: Soulangia thymifolia, also known as Mexican valerian, is a perennial plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Valerianaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including the alleviation of insomnia, anxiety, and muscle spasms, attributed to its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The bioactive constituents of Soulangia thymifolia include valerenic acid, isovalerenic acid, and valepotriates, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Soulangia thymifolia include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to support sleep and reduce anxiety.
